# One word per line. Blank lines and lines starting with # are ignored.
# Words listed here are NOT flagged by codespell. Grouped by reason.

# ===== File structure / source markers =====

# comment marker <!-- splitted from ./en/... --> (origin of a split file)
splitted


# ===== Protocol / SQL keywords =====

# SQL/PDO keyword (IN/OUT/INOUT for stored procedures)
inout
# valid FTP command (RFC 959, pre-allocate space)
allo


# ===== British / valid hyphenated spellings =====

co-ordinate
co-ordinates
re-use
re-used
re-usable
re-using


# ===== Foreign words appearing in multilingual example output =====

# German pronoun "Sie"
sie
# "Dezember" = December in German
dezember
# "Februar" = February in German (date_format output with DE locale)
februar
# French feminine adjective ("heure normale du Japon")
normale
# German "Adresse" (= Address, in a German socket error message)
adresse


# ===== Proper nouns / cities / products =====

# Nome, Alaska — part of America/Nome (real IANA timezone)
nome
# Herat, Afghanistan — real city (mysqli example "Herat (AFG)")
herat
# Russian cryptographic standard
gost
# Empress Database (historical product)
empress
# arbitrary dog name in an SPL example
mavrick
# Vai writing system (PCRE/Unicode table)
vai
# Hald CLUT, color lookup technique in image processing
hald
# IBM i PASE (Portable Application Solutions Environment)
pase
# SystemTap CLI tool name (<filename>stap</filename>)
stap


# ===== Abbreviations / short example strings =====

# abbreviation ("2nd") or chemistry (Nd = Neodymium)
nd
# regex fragment with embedded comment: /te(?#...)st/
te
# dummy string in enchant example ('mis' -> 'cor')
mis
# short variable name: $ser = serialize(...)
ser
# stripslashes array example fragment: "fo\\'o"
fo
# $parms variable name in yaz example (arbitrary, renaming = scope creep)
parms


# ===== Example outputs (arbitrary string results) =====

# output of str_increment("ABC") -> "ABD"
abd
# output of str_split("Hello", 3) -> ["Hel", "lo"]
hel
# trim output that leaves "Worl"
worl
# dummy string in json-encode example ('&blong&')
blong
# rar archive example file name (tese.txt)
tese


# ===== Valid technical terms =====

# "making lighter" (opposite of darkening) -- correct image processing term
lightening


# ===== Untouchable identifiers (xml:id, variables, PHP keywords) =====

# xml:id="language.references.arent" (apostrophe not allowed in an id)
arent
# xml:id="regexp.reference.onlyonce" (space not allowed in an id)
onlyonce
# xml:id="constant.swoole-error-server-send-to-woker-timeout" (renaming = broken link)
woker
# made-up function in a PHP example (function fot(...)) -- "for" is a PHP keyword
fot
# $fpr variable in a mongodb example (renaming to $for clashes with a keyword)
fpr
# 6-char chunk in segmented output of db2-lob-read example ("Loop 3: T. THI")
thi
# capture group in PCRE regex (sens|respons)e and \1ibility (renaming breaks the regex)
respons
# xml:id="filters.covert.quoted-printable" (stable anchor, renaming would break external links)
covert


# ===== Accepted English variants =====

# PHP $statics variable name (handy plural for "static variables")
statics
# accepted variant of "implementers"
implementors
# used throughout the PHP manual (invokable object / __invoke)
invokable
# technically wrong in modern English (data is invariable), kept on cubrid pending review
datas


# ===== Untouchable legal text (appendices/license.xml) =====

synching
merchantibility
